operational_knowledge_units.title: Stunnel connections view should display three connections during unplanned restart check
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide indicates that in stunnel, using File > Connections should show three connections: AI01, AI02, and SortationChat.
observed_evidence: Visible text: 'It should show 3 connections AI01, AI02, and SortationChat.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Services / Unplanned restart. Open stunnel. Under File click Connections. The connections view should show 3 connections: AI01, AI02, and SortationChat.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Check stunnel connections during unplanned restart
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide titled 'Services / Unplanned restart' instructs the user to open stunnel, select File > Connections, and verify three connections are shown: AI01, AI02, and SortationChat.

steps.visual_region_hint: stunnel application referenced by slide text

steps.instruction: In stunnel, click File, then click Connections.
steps.expected_outcome: The connections view or dialog opens.
steps.validation_check: A stunnel connections listing is displayed.
steps.visual_region_hint: File menu and Connections option in stunnel

steps.instruction: Verify that the connections shown are AI01, AI02, and SortationChat.
steps.expected_outcome: Three connections are visible: AI01, AI02, and SortationChat.
steps.validation_check: Exactly these three connection names are shown.

procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Validation check for unplanned restart: open stunnel, go to File > Connections, and confirm the three connections AI01, AI02, and SortationChat are present.
